headers.comment: Security headers on EVERY path. Firebase matches header globs against the REQUEST path, not the rewritten file — so the root '/' and SPA routes need '**', not '**/*.html'. Cache-Control here defaults documents to no-cache; the asset block below overrides it for fingerprinted static files.

headers.value: geolocation=(self), accelerometer=(self), magnetometer=(self), gyroscope=(self), microphone=(), camera=(), payment=(), usb=()

default-src 'self'; script-src 'self' 'wasm-unsafe-eval' 'unsafe-inline' https://www.gstatic.com; style-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline'; img-src 'self' data: blob:; font-src 'self' https://fonts.gstatic.com; connect-src 'self' https://www.gstatic.com https://fonts.gstatic.com; worker-src 'self' blob:; frame-ancestors 'none'; base-uri 'self'; form-action 'none'; object-src 'none'

headers.comment: Fingerprinted static assets can be cached forever (overrides the no-cache above because this block is listed later).
Service worker must never be cached.
